name: Honda Accord (8th Gen)
engine: 2.4L DOHC i-VTEC I4 (K24Z), 177–190 hp — 3.5L SOHC i-VTEC V6 (J35Z) with VCM, 271 hp
transmission: 5-speed automatic or 5-speed manual (2.4L) · 5-speed automatic or 6-speed manual (V6 coupe)
reliability: 8/10 — a roomy, durable sedan, with three documented things to check. The V6 uses cylinder deactivation (VCM), which can drive oil consumption and motor-mount wear (owners often fit a VCM disabler). Many 2008–2010 cars had premature front brake wear and judder, which Honda addressed through service bulletins. And this generation falls under the industry-wide Takata airbag recall. The 2.4L I4 is the simpler, trouble-free pick.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Low to average · Maintenance ~$400/yr (V6 a little more) · Cheap to run with strong resale
popular_mods: VCM disabler on V6 cars to cut oil burn and motor-mount wear
All-weather liners and tasteful wheels with quality tires
OEM-plus suspension refresh on higher-mileage cars
Honest take: the 2.4L I4 is the no-drama choice; the V6 coupe with the 6-speed manual is the quiet enthusiast pick
oil_type: 0W-20 (V6) / 5W-20 (early I4) — confirm by engine and year

generations.8th Gen (2008–2012): Grew into a roomy, near-full-size sedan (plus a coupe). 2.4L K24 four (177hp LX / 190hp EX) or a 271hp 3.5L J35 V6 with cylinder deactivation. Comfortable, quiet, and easy to live with.
generations.2011 refresh: Mild front and rear styling updates plus equipment changes; powertrains carried over.

common_issues: V6 VCM-related oil consumption and rear motor-mount wear (idle vibration as it ages)
2008–2010 premature front brake wear and judder — Honda issued service bulletins
Takata airbag-inflator recall (this generation is included) — verify completion by VIN
Occasional power-steering pump whine on high-mileage cars
maintenance_tips: Many V6 owners fit a VCM disabler; check oil level between changes regardless
Inspect front brakes and rotors on a 2008–2010 car
Confirm the Takata airbag recall is complete
Service the automatic with genuine Honda ATF; inspect motor mounts if idle vibration appears
